pnpm run generate-maintainer-docs-from-ai && pnpm run check && pnpm test

tasks.title: [defect] Add pre-persist gap review for UX and CAE task batches
tasks.approach: Implement a dry-run review step in the planning/task creation flow after the draft-normalization behavior from T974 is fixed. The gate should validate the normalized task-batch shape for UX/CAE work: coverage of state behavior, mutation safety, rollback, empty/first-run states, styling, verification, and right-sized task boundaries before persistence.
technicalScope: Define a UX/CAE task-batch quality checklist covering refresh behavior, empty/first-run states, rollback/activation, draft preview, visual hierarchy, disabled states, and verification artifacts.
Add a dry-run review command/path for planning outputs or persist-planning-execution-drafts payloads before persistence.
Return structured findings for oversized tasks, missing domain slices, missing verification tasks, and unclear acceptance criteria.
Update planning guidance so agents run the review before persisting ready UX/CAE task batches.
Add tests using a deficient batch that triggers findings and a complete batch that passes.
acceptanceCriteria: A UX/CAE batch review produces machine-readable findings before any task rows are persisted.
The review flags at least oversized tasks, missing verification coverage, missing rollback/activation slice, and missing empty/first-run behavior when absent.
A complete batch can pass the review and then persist without changing the task payload semantics.
Planning docs and examples show the review-before-persist sequence.
Tests prove the review reduces the specific corrective-pass failure from the transcript.
tasks.summary: Add a pre-persist quality gate for UX/CAE task batches so agents split oversized work and catch missing slices before rows become ready.
tasks.description: Implementation intent: structural validation only proves tasks are syntactically workable. UX/CAE batches need a domain checklist, but that checklist must inspect the same normalized draft shape that persist-planning-execution-drafts will persist; therefore this task depends on T974.
tasks.risk: Ready queues can contain incomplete or oversized work batches that require user-prompted repair before implementation can safely start.

rootCauseAnalysis.findingsSummary: Task creation supports persistence of arbitrary workable rows, but there is no domain-specific decomposition quality gate before ready tasks are written.
rootCauseAnalysis.rootCause: The planning module can produce multi-task draft previews, and persist-planning-execution-drafts can write them, but neither enforces a domain checklist for UX/CAE work. Manual task creation can skip a pre-persist gap review entirely. The system validates that tasks are structurally workable, not that the batch covers refresh behavior, rollback, draft preview, first-run UX, disabled states, or verification slices.
rootCauseAnalysis.recommendation: Add a pre-persist task-batch review step for UX/CAE phases. It can be a dry-run validator or planning checklist that flags oversized tasks, missing operational slices, missing verification tasks, and obvious domain gaps before rows become ready.
rootCauseAnalysis.architecturalConsiderations: Add domain-aware planning validators. The task engine should retain generic structural validation, while planning modules provide optional quality gates keyed by work type/component.

tasks.title: [defect] Stabilize phase command response shape for agent summarizers
tasks.approach: Add a typed response projection for phase commands after T976 settles the domain rollover result shape. The raw command data remains the source of truth; T980 owns only data.presentation fields for summarizers across dry-run, live write, idempotent replay, and follow-up-needed outcomes.
technicalScope: Define data.presentation.phaseRollover or equivalent typed projection for set-current-phase and related phase commands using the T976 domain result fields.
Populate beforePhase, afterPhase, workspaceRevisionBefore/After, exportStatus, configHintStatus, suggestedFollowUpCommand, and optional taskCounts.
Ensure the projection is present for dry-run, live mutation, and idempotent replay responses.
Update response-template docs/contracts and any extension/dashboard consumers that summarize phase command results.
Add tests that assert the stable presentation shape for success, dry-run, replay, and follow-up-needed cases.
acceptanceCriteria: Agent summarizers can read the same documented presentation fields for phase commands across dry-run, live write, and idempotent replay.
The presentation object is derived from T976 domain result data and does not introduce separate mutation semantics.
When export/status follow-up is needed, the presentation object includes a machine-readable follow-up command.
Tests fail if the presentation shape changes without contract updates.
Docs identify the presentation object as the supported summarizer contract and distinguish it from raw command result data.
tasks.summary: Expose a stable phase rollover presentation object for agent summaries, consuming the domain result data produced by T976.
tasks.description: Implementation intent: a summarizer failure after a successful mutation is dangerous because the agent may not know whether to retry. This task should not change rollover mutation semantics; it should define a compact, stable view model over the T976 domain result.
tasks.risk: Agents can face-plant after successful state changes and retry or summarize incorrectly if response shapes are command-specific and undocumented.

rootCauseAnalysis.findingsSummary: The response-template layer identifies phase-ship commands, but it does not provide a stable summarizer-specific phase rollover projection.
rootCauseAnalysis.rootCause: phase_ship only declares generic expected sections such as evidence, planningGeneration, snapshotBefore, fileRelativePath, and dryRun. set-current-phase returns workspaceStatusBefore/After, config hints, canonicalPhase, exportStatus, and suggestedFollowUpCommand. The template reports matchedSections but does not normalize these into a stable presentation shape, so summarizers can guess wrong.
rootCauseAnalysis.recommendation: Add an explicit data.presentation.phaseShip (or phaseRollover) object for set-current-phase with stable fields: before phase, after phase, workspace revisions, export status, config match, task counts if available, and required follow-up.
rootCauseAnalysis.architecturalConsiderations: Move response templates from section-presence hints toward typed presentation contracts for high-risk workflows. Agent-facing summaries should consume stable view models, not raw command-specific payloads.

tasks.title: [defect] Separate volatile kit runtime state from tracked planning state
tasks.approach: Treat tracked planning SQLite as durable source-of-truth state only. Move CAE/runtime traces to an untracked runtime store or gate them behind explicit persistence, then give release agents a deterministic classifier for dirty kit-owned files before checkout, merge, tag, or publish operations.
technicalScope: Inventory writers to .workspace-kit/tasks/workspace-kit.db, especially CAE trace persistence and read-ish command preflights.
Move volatile CAE traces/runtime telemetry to an untracked runtime DB/log or make persistence opt-in/disabled for release flows.
Reduce unnecessary whole-store rewrites for small task/workspace mutations where practical.
Add a kit-state classifier command or release preflight that labels dirty kit-owned files as durable state, generated export, or volatile runtime churn.
Update release playbooks with deterministic actions for each classification before checkout, pull, merge, tag, and publish.
acceptanceCriteria: Read-only workspace-kit commands and CAE shadow preflight do not dirty tracked planning SQLite by default during release workflows.
If a command intentionally mutates tracked planning SQLite, its response explains whether the change is durable evidence and should be committed.
A release preflight/classifier reports kit-owned dirty files with safe next actions before branch-sensitive Git operations.
Tests cover CAE/read-only command behavior, intentional durable mutation behavior, and classifier output for known dirty-file categories.
Release guidance documents how to recover when a remote PR merged but local sync is blocked by kit-owned state.
tasks.summary: Separate durable tracked planning state from volatile runtime telemetry, and add release tooling that classifies kit-owned Git dirtiness safely.
tasks.description: Implementation intent: routine read/preflight commands should not mutate tracked release state. When tracked kit files are dirty, agents need to know whether the changes are durable task/workspace evidence, generated export drift, or disposable runtime telemetry before they stash, commit, or regenerate anything.
tasks.risk: Volatile writes to tracked SQLite can block branch operations and make release evidence look haunted as hell.

rootCauseAnalysis.findingsSummary: Combined T978/T979: tracked planning SQLite is used for durable task state and volatile CAE/runtime traces, which dirties Git and blocks release branch operations. Resolve by separating volatile runtime state and adding release Git recovery/classification.
rootCauseAnalysis.rootCause: CAE shadow preflight persists trace snapshots into the same kit SQLite file used for tracked task/workspace state. Many workspace-kit commands include CAE preflight, so even read-ish commands can write trace rows and mutate the tracked DB. In addition, task persistence rewrites the relational task table from the in-memory document on save, increasing byte churn.
rootCauseAnalysis.recommendation:
Combined defect scope from T978 and T979.

Findings: SQLite byte churn and local Git blockage share one underlying persistence boundary problem.

Root cause: CAE shadow preflight persists trace snapshots into the same tracked SQLite file that stores task/workspace authority. Many workspace-kit commands run CAE preflight, so read-ish commands can dirty the tracked DB. When that happens during release operations, branch switches, pulls, tags, and remote merge sync can be blocked by generated-state churn.

Recommendation: Move CAE traces and other volatile runtime telemetry to an untracked runtime DB/log or disable persistence during release flows unless explicitly requested. Add a release Git-state preflight/recovery command that classifies kit-owned DB changes as durable state, generated export, or disposable runtime churn.

Higher-level architecture: Split durable planning source of truth from runtime observability. Add a VCS-aware kit state classifier for release workflows.
rootCauseAnalysis.architecturalConsiderations: Separate durable planning state from volatile observability and expose a VCS-aware state classifier.

combinedDefect.rationale: Root-cause metadata showed overlapping subsystem/root cause and shared resolution path.

tasks.title: [defect] Make phase rollover, workspace status, and export updates atomic
tasks.approach: Extend set-current-phase or add a rollover-phase command that updates the canonical phase, focus text, pending decisions, next actions, blockers, config hints, and export together. T976 owns the domain mutation and raw result data; T980 owns the typed presentation projection consumed by summarizers.
technicalScope: Design the rollover command/input shape for currentKitPhase, nextKitPhase, label/focus, blockers, pendingDecisions, nextAgentActions, export behavior, and idempotency.
Implement the rollover as a single SQLite workspace-status transaction plus config/export synchronization where appropriate.
Change export freshness to compare workspace-status revision or status-event timestamp instead of the entire planning SQLite file mtime.

Return authoritative domain result data with before/after status, revisions, export status, config hint status, and follow-up command if needed, without defining the summarizer presentation projection.
Update phase-status, set-current-phase docs, and tests for stale text cleanup and export freshness.
acceptanceCriteria: A normal phase rollover clears or replaces previous-phase pending decisions and next actions in the same CLI flow.
The compatibility export is fresh after the rollover, or the command returns an explicit required follow-up in its JSON response.
phase-status does not mark export stale solely because unrelated CAE/task writes changed the SQLite file mtime.
Tests cover stale previous-phase text, export freshness after workspace-status mutation, and idempotent retry of the rollover command.
Docs distinguish the rollover domain result contract owned by this task from the presentation projection owned by T980.
tasks.summary: Make phase rollover an intent-level workspace-status aggregate update with clean domain result data, stale text cleanup, and export freshness in one auditable flow.
tasks.description: Implementation intent: phase rollover is one operator workflow even though today it is split across set-current-phase, update-workspace-status, and export-workspace-status. Agents should receive authoritative domain result data that says what changed, whether the export is fresh, and what follow-up remains. The summarizer-specific data.presentation contract is intentionally left to T980.
tasks.risk: Split phase/status/export commands leave stale previous-phase guidance and false export-staleness warnings after normal workflows.

rootCauseAnalysis.findingsSummary: Combined T976/T977: phase rollover is split across commands, and export freshness is tied to whole-DB mtime. Resolve by making workspace-status rollover an intent-level aggregate and basing export freshness on workspace-status revision rather than planning DB mtime.
rootCauseAnalysis.rootCause: set-current-phase only patches currentKitPhase, lastUpdated, optional nextKitPhase, and optional activeFocus. It does not accept or derive pendingDecisions, nextAgentActions, or blockers, and update-workspace-status is the separate low-level patch path. As a result, phase-specific prose from the prior phase remains unless the agent performs a second command.
rootCauseAnalysis.recommendation:
Combined defect scope from T976 and T977.

Findings: Phase rollover, workspace status cleanup, and export freshness are one operator workflow but are currently split across set-current-phase, update-workspace-status, and export-workspace-status.

Root cause: set-current-phase only updates phase/focus fields and writes the export once. Follow-up status cleanup requires update-workspace-status, which does not refresh the export. phase-status then compares export mtime against the entire planning DB mtime, so unrelated CAE/task writes can report the export as stale.

Recommendation: Extend set-current-phase or add a rollover-phase command that updates current phase, focus, pending decisions, next actions, blockers, config hints, and export together. Base export freshness on workspaceStatus.workspaceRevision or workspace-status audit timestamp.

Higher-level architecture: Treat workspace status as a domain aggregate with its own revision/export contract. Do not use whole SQLite file mtime as a cross-domain freshness signal.
rootCauseAnalysis.architecturalConsiderations: Make workspace status a first-class aggregate with revisioned exports and intent-level mutations.

combinedDefect.rationale: Root-cause metadata showed overlapping subsystem/root cause and shared resolution path.

review.summary: Reviewed changed files and PR scope before merge; local validation and GitHub CI passed.

tasks.title: [defect] Make JSON stdout parsing hard to misuse in agent workflows
tasks.approach: Treat machine-readable CLI execution as its own contract. Standardize examples and tests on pnpm exec wk or a dedicated clean-json wrapper, and make common parse failures actionable when wrapper banners contaminate stdout.
technicalScope: Audit agent-facing examples in .ai instructions, task-engine instructions, scripts, and tests for parse-sensitive pnpm run wk usage.
Replace parse-sensitive examples with pnpm exec wk, node dist/cli.js, or a dedicated clean-json helper.
Add a helper script/function for tests and agents that captures stdout, parses the full JSON document, and separates stderr diagnostics.
Add targeted remediation text for parse failures that appear to include pnpm/package-manager banner output.
Add tests that demonstrate clean JSON parsing and banner-contaminated failure remediation.
acceptanceCriteria: All parse-sensitive docs and test snippets use a banner-free invocation pattern.
A reusable helper exists for parsing workspace-kit JSON output without line-splitting assumptions.
Banner-contaminated stdout yields a remediation that tells agents to use pnpm exec wk or node dist/cli.js.
Tests cover pretty-printed multi-line JSON and wrapper-banner contamination.
No agent-facing example instructs line-based JSON parsing for workspace-kit run output.
tasks.summary: Provide a banner-free machine JSON invocation path and targeted remediation for pnpm wrapper output that breaks parsers.
tasks.description: Implementation intent: workspace-kit already emits one JSON value, but agents often run it through wrappers that add banner text. The project should make the safe invocation obvious, tested, and available as a reusable helper rather than relying on agents remembering the footgun.
tasks.risk: Agents may misclassify successful mutations as parse failures or retry unsafe commands when stdout is polluted by package-manager banners.

rootCauseAnalysis.findingsSummary: The CLI JSON contract is clean for workspace-kit itself, but wrapper commands can add non-JSON stdout that breaks agent scripts.
workflowParts: .ai/AGENT-CLI-MAP.md Shell scripts and JSON stdout section
agent verification scripts that parse stdout
rootCauseAnalysis.rootCause: workspace-kit run emits one JSON value, but pnpm run may prepend package-manager script banners. The docs warn to use pnpm exec for parse-sensitive scripts, but examples and habits still mix pnpm run and pnpm exec. The failure is a discoverability/ergonomics bug more than a runtime serialization bug.
rootCauseAnalysis.recommendation: Standardize parse-sensitive examples on pnpm exec wk or node dist/cli.js. Add a small helper/wrapper for 'clean JSON' command execution and make JSON parse failures caused by pnpm banners produce a targeted remediation.
rootCauseAnalysis.architecturalConsiderations: Treat machine-readable CLI invocation as a separate contract from human-friendly npm scripts. Agent automation should have a stable, banner-free entrypoint and tests that assert stdout is parseable JSON.

review.summary: Reviewed changed files and PR scope before merge; local validation and GitHub CI passed.

tasks.title: [defect] Align bulk task validation with target phase override behavior
tasks.approach: Separate draft normalization from final TaskEntity validation. Apply targetPhaseKey, targetPhase, desiredStatus, provenance, and allocated defaults to each draft row before calling the shared conversion/validation path, then update schemas/docs/tests to match the actual accepted payload.
technicalScope: Refactor persist-planning-execution-drafts to add a draft-normalization step before buildTaskFromConversionPayload or equivalent final validation.
Ensure targetPhaseKey, targetPhase, desiredStatus, planRef, planningType, and per-row overrides have deterministic precedence.
Update schema-only sample args and instruction examples to show a working batch payload without redundant per-row phase fields.
Add regression tests for targetPhaseKey + targetPhase + desiredStatus with tasks[] rows that omit phase.
Add negative tests for conflicting row-level and command-level phase/status values if conflicts are rejected or documented.
acceptanceCriteria: A documented batch payload using only command-level targetPhaseKey/targetPhase and desiredStatus successfully persists tasks with correct phase fields.
If conflicting command-level and row-level defaults are provided, the result follows documented precedence or returns a structured invalid-task-schema error.
Schema-only output and instruction examples match the working payload used in tests.
No partial task rows are persisted when validation fails.
Regression coverage proves the first-attempt failure pattern from the transcript no longer occurs.
tasks.summary: Fix persist-planning-execution-drafts so command-level phase/status defaults are normalized before per-row validation, or fail with a clear preflight contract.
tasks.description: Implementation intent: the bulk task command advertises batch-level defaults, so agents should not need redundant per-row phase fields when targetPhase/targetPhaseKey are present. If the project chooses not to support that shorthand, the command must reject it early with schema/sample guidance before agents build invalid payloads.
tasks.risk: Leaky validation order creates avoidable failed writes and fragile retries during task-batch creation.

rootCauseAnalysis.findingsSummary: The bulk task command's user-facing batch defaults are applied after row validation, so the advertised targetPhase convenience is leaky.
rootCauseAnalysis.rootCause: persist-planning-execution-drafts calls buildTaskFromConversionPayload(row) before applying targetPhaseKey, targetPhase, and desiredStatus. buildTaskFromConversionPayload requires row.phase for 'workable tasks', so a payload that relies on the command-level targetPhase fails before the override can help.
rootCauseAnalysis.recommendation: Normalize each row with command-level defaults before calling the shared task builder, or refactor the builder to accept default context for phase/status. Update schema-only sample args and instruction examples, and add a regression test for targetPhaseKey + targetPhase + desiredStatus without per-row phase.
rootCauseAnalysis.architecturalConsiderations: Separate task draft normalization from task validation. Batch-level defaults, id allocation, and provenance should be resolved in a draft-normalization layer before the final TaskEntity validator runs.

review.summary: Reviewed changed files and PR diff scope before merge; CI passed on GitHub.

tasks.title: [defect] Create machine-readable release evidence, approval, and follow-up manifest
tasks.approach: Add a release evidence artifact and CLI path that agents can produce, validate, and cite during phase closeout. The manifest should reuse the task-delivery evidence vocabulary from T971, while dirty-state classification from T978 remains a related release-hardening input rather than a hard prerequisite.
technicalScope: Define a versioned release evidence manifest schema with releaseVersion, packageName, git refs, approval, changelog/releaseNotes, validations, risks, publishArtifacts, and followUpTasks.
Add CLI support to create/read/validate the manifest during phase closeout, or extend an existing release/phase command to emit it.
Record publish approval separately from run policyApproval while preserving actor, timestamp, rationale, and scope.
Update phase closeout and release guidance to require manifest citation before tag/npm/GitHub release actions.
Add tests for manifest validation, missing approval, missing changelog evidence, and follow-up task reconciliation.
acceptanceCriteria: A release agent can run a CLI command and receive a JSON release evidence manifest before publish.
The manifest includes explicit human approval details or reports a structured missing-approval failure.
The manifest includes changelog/release-note evidence through an agent-readable path without requiring direct human-doc scraping.
The final phase summary renders follow-up task counts from the manifest and cannot claim zero follow-ups without a recorded friction scan or rationale.
Tests cover successful manifest creation plus failures for missing approval, missing release notes, and unresolved follow-up scan state.
tasks.summary: Create a machine-readable release evidence manifest that unifies approval, changelog/release notes, validations, known risks, publish proof, and follow-up tasks.
tasks.description: Implementation intent: release closeout needs a durable aggregate similar to policy traces. It should capture the human approval artifact, release scope, validation commands/results, changelog or release-note evidence, migration/risk notes, publish proof, and linked follow-up work. This task depends on T971 for shared delivery-evidence vocabulary; it should coordinate with T978 only where release evidence wants to cite dirty-state classification.
tasks.risk: Publish approval and release evidence can otherwise live only in chat/prose, making release audits ambiguous when transcript export or doc access is incomplete.

rootCauseAnalysis.findingsSummary: Combined T972/T973/T981: publish approval, changelog evidence, known risks, and follow-up tasks are release evidence but live in chat/human docs/counts. Resolve with a machine-readable release evidence manifest that agents can cite before publish and final summary.
rootCauseAnalysis.rootCause: Publish approval currently lives in chat procedure: the agent presents a summary and waits for explicit human confirmation. That approval is not a structured workspace-kit command, not a policy trace, and not attached to a release evidence record. If Cursor export omits the user's confirmation or an approval UI event, the transcript can show 'Approval received' with no auditable source.
rootCauseAnalysis.recommendation:
Combined defect scope from T972, T973, and T981.

Findings: Release closeout lacks one durable machine-readable evidence aggregate for approval, changelog/migration notes, known risks, and follow-up tasks.

Root cause: publish approval is captured procedurally in chat, changelog evidence points at maintainer prose that agents may not read routinely, and phase summary follow-on counts come from task-store counts without a mandatory transcript/friction reconciliation step. These are all pieces of release evidence, but they are not modeled as one artifact.

Recommendation: Add a release evidence manifest/command containing release version, scope, approval artifact, approver/timestamp, changelog or release-note entry, migration/security notes, validation artifacts, known risks, and linked follow-up tasks. Require agents to cite this manifest before tagging/publishing and to render final summaries from it.

Higher-level architecture: Treat release evidence as a first-class aggregate generated from task state, package metadata, changelog source, validation artifacts, and approval records.
rootCauseAnalysis.architecturalConsiderations: Create a release evidence aggregate that unifies approval, changelog, validation, risks, and follow-up links.

combinedDefect.rationale: Root-cause metadata showed overlapping subsystem/root cause and shared resolution path.

tasks.title: [defect] Require branch and PR evidence before completing phase tasks
tasks.approach: Implement this as a Task Engine and playbook enforcement improvement, not just more prose. Define the delivery-evidence fields the engine can inspect, add a preflight/guard path for phase task completion, and update the phase delivery playbooks so agents know exactly which evidence must exist before completing task rows.
technicalScope: Define a task delivery-evidence metadata contract for branch name, PR URL/number, base branch, merge SHA, required check names/results, validation commands, and optional waiver fields.
Add a phase-delivery preflight or run-transition completion guard that evaluates delivery evidence for execution tasks in the active phase.
Update task-to-phase and phase-closeout playbooks to require the same evidence fields before completing phase-delivery tasks.
Add tests for complete-with-evidence, complete-with-waiver, missing-evidence violation output, and non-shipping/local-only task behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: A read-only preflight lists phase tasks that are completed or about to be completed without PR/merge/check evidence or a waiver.
The completion path blocks or emits a structured violation for phase-delivery tasks missing required evidence, according to the configured enforcement mode.
A maintainer waiver requires actor, rationale, timestamp, and scope, and appears in task evidence or preflight output.
Playbook examples show the exact metadata fields and CLI sequence agents should use before task completion.
Automated tests cover evidence present, evidence missing, waiver present, and unrelated non-phase tasks.
tasks.summary: Add an evidence-backed completion gate so phase-delivery tasks cannot be marked complete without PR/merge/check evidence or an explicit maintainer waiver.
tasks.description: Implementation intent: completion for shipping phase work should be an evidence-backed transition. The system should be able to tell whether a task has linked branch/PR/merge/check evidence, or whether a maintainer explicitly waived that requirement. The guard can start advisory if needed, but the contract must be machine-readable and test-covered.
tasks.risk: Without a machine-checkable completion evidence contract, agents can make task state look shipped before GitHub review, merge, or CI evidence exists.

rootCauseAnalysis.findingsSummary: The reported failure is not a task-engine transition bug; it is a governance/enforcement gap between phase delivery playbooks and task lifecycle commands.
workflowParts: task-engine task rows and transition evidence
GitHub PR/merge evidence for release/phase-* branches
rootCauseAnalysis.rootCause: The playbooks require branch, PR, review, merge, and then task completion, but run-transition only enforces lifecycle state and policyApproval. It does not know whether a task has PR evidence, a release/phase branch merge, CI status, or a maintainer waiver. The single-task playbook is also easier to apply to one T### than to a broad 'complete all phase tasks' instruction, so an agent can satisfy local validation and then complete rows before Git evidence exists.
rootCauseAnalysis.recommendation: Add a phase-wide delivery path and guard: before completing execution tasks for a shipping phase, require either PR/merge evidence metadata (PR URL, base branch, merge SHA, checks) or an explicit waiver recorded on the task or phase closeout artifact. Update run-transition/complete guidance and consider a closeout preflight that blocks when completed rows lack delivery evidence.
rootCauseAnalysis.architecturalConsiderations: Introduce a first-class delivery evidence model that links task lifecycle state to VCS artifacts and CI results. Treat task completion as an evidence-backed state transition, not just a row status change.

tasks.title: [defect] Provide machine-safe changelog evidence path for release agents
tasks.approach: Do not implement this separately. Preserve the transcript provenance here and implement machine-safe changelog/release-note evidence in T972 as part of the release manifest.
technicalScope: No standalone implementation for T981.
Verify T972 includes machine-readable changelog or release-note evidence.
Keep this row cancelled as provenance for the merged defect scope.
acceptanceCriteria: T972 acceptance criteria include changelog/release-note evidence through an agent-readable path.
T972 covers the original blocked-human-doc evidence failure mode.
This cancelled row remains visibly superseded by T972.
tasks.summary: Cancelled and superseded by T972; machine-safe changelog evidence is now part of the release evidence manifest.
tasks.description: Superseded task record. T972 owns the implementation because changelog evidence, approval, validation, risk, and follow-up links should be one release evidence aggregate.
tasks.risk: No standalone risk remains if T972 includes agent-readable release-note/changelog evidence.

rootCauseAnalysis.findingsSummary: Release evidence requires changelog confirmation, but the agent-facing canon mostly points to human docs that agents are told not to read routinely.
rootCauseAnalysis.rootCause: .ai/RELEASING.md names docs/maintainers/CHANGELOG.md as the changelog gate, while agent-doc-routing discourages routine reads under docs/maintainers. The root CHANGELOG is pointer-only and contains only the latest release summary. There is no machine-readable release-note evidence surface that agents can use when docs are filtered or intentionally avoided.
rootCauseAnalysis.recommendation: Provide a machine-safe release evidence surface, such as .ai/release-notes.jsonl, .ai/CHANGELOG.md, or a CLI command that returns the relevant changelog entry and migration notes. Update release playbooks to use that surface before falling back to human docs.
rootCauseAnalysis.architecturalConsiderations: Create a release manifest/evidence object generated from canonical changelog, package metadata, task scope, and validation artifacts. Agents should validate releases against that manifest rather than scraping human prose.

metadata.supersededReason: Combined with lead task after root-cause metadata review showed overlapping subsystem/root cause and a shared resolution path.

tasks.title: [defect] Prevent local DB churn from obscuring remote PR merge state
tasks.approach: Do not implement this separately. Keep the transcript provenance here and implement the recovery path in T978, where it can share the same state-classification machinery.
technicalScope: No standalone implementation for T979.
Verify T978 includes remote PR merged/local dirty recovery guidance.
Keep this row cancelled as provenance for the merged defect scope.
acceptanceCriteria: T978 acceptance criteria include a release preflight/classifier before branch-sensitive Git operations.
T978 documentation covers remote merge succeeded but local checkout/pull is blocked by kit-owned state.
This cancelled row remains visibly superseded by T978.
tasks.summary: Cancelled and superseded by T978; remote-merge/local-dirty recovery is part of the kit-state classifier and volatile-state split.
tasks.description: Superseded task record. T978 owns the implementation because local merge recovery depends on distinguishing durable task state from generated or volatile kit churn.
tasks.risk: No standalone risk remains if T978 includes release preflight and remote-merge recovery guidance.

rootCauseAnalysis.findingsSummary: Local generated-state churn can block Git operations after a remote merge has already succeeded, leaving agents in a split-brain release state.
rootCauseAnalysis.rootCause: The release flow depends on Git branch switches and pulls, but the tracked SQLite file can become locally dirty from routine kit commands. There is no pre-merge/pre-checkout guard that classifies the DB diff as authoritative state vs disposable runtime churn. Once GitHub merges remotely, the local repo may still be unable to sync cleanly.
rootCauseAnalysis.recommendation: Add a release git-state preflight and recovery path: before merge/tag/pull, classify kit-owned DB changes, back them up if needed, and either commit, regenerate, or discard them through an approved command. Document the exact recovery when the remote PR merge succeeded but local sync is blocked.
rootCauseAnalysis.architecturalConsiderations: Introduce a VCS-aware kit state classifier. It should know which kit-owned changes are source-of-truth mutations, generated exports, or volatile runtime artifacts, and expose safe actions for each category.

metadata.supersededReason: Combined with lead task after root-cause metadata review showed overlapping subsystem/root cause and a shared resolution path.

tasks.title: [defect] Keep workspace status export fresh after status cleanup
tasks.approach: Do not implement this separately. Keep this row as provenance and implement the export freshness fix as part of T976 so phase, status text, and export consistency are solved together.
technicalScope: No standalone implementation for T977.
Verify T976 includes export freshness semantics and status mutation/export synchronization.
Keep this row cancelled as provenance for the merged defect scope.
acceptanceCriteria: T976 acceptance criteria include automatic or explicitly reported export freshness after workspace-status changes.
T976 covers the original update-workspace-status plus export drift failure mode.
This cancelled row remains visibly superseded by T976.
tasks.summary: Cancelled and superseded by T976; export freshness is now covered by the phase rollover aggregate task.
tasks.description: Superseded task record. T976 owns the implementation because export freshness and stale status cleanup share the same workspace-status aggregate boundary.
tasks.risk: No standalone risk remains if T976 bases freshness on workspace-status revision and refreshes/export-reports during rollover.

rootCauseAnalysis.findingsSummary: Export freshness is measured against the whole planning DB mtime, but unrelated writes can touch the DB after the export.
rootCauseAnalysis.rootCause: phase-status reports export staleness by comparing docs/maintainers/data/workspace-kit-status.db-export.yaml mtime with the planning SQLite file mtime. The planning DB contains more than workspace status; CAE trace writes and other planning-store writes can update the DB mtime without changing kit_workspace_status. update-workspace-status also does not automatically refresh the export.
rootCauseAnalysis.recommendation: Base export freshness on workspaceStatus.workspaceRevision or the workspace-status audit event timestamp, not the entire DB file mtime. Refresh the export automatically after workspace-status mutations or include a precise follow-up when only workspace-status fields changed.
rootCauseAnalysis.architecturalConsiderations: Stop using whole-file SQLite mtime as a cross-domain freshness signal. Track per-domain revisions for task state, workspace status, CAE traces, and exports.

metadata.supersededReason: Combined with lead task after root-cause metadata review showed overlapping subsystem/root cause and a shared resolution path.

tasks.title: [defect] Force release-friction follow-up scan before reporting zero follow-on tasks
tasks.approach: Do not implement this as a standalone task. Preserve the transcript provenance here, but direct execution to T972 so release approval, changelog evidence, risks, and follow-up scans are solved as one manifest-backed workflow.
technicalScope: No standalone implementation for T973.
Verify T972 includes release-friction scan fields and follow-up task reconciliation.
Keep this row cancelled as provenance for the merged defect scope.
acceptanceCriteria: T972 acceptance criteria include a friction scan or explicit zero-follow-up rationale before final summary.
T972 implementation preserves the original problem: summaries must not hide observed release friction.
This cancelled row remains visibly superseded by T972.
tasks.summary: Cancelled and superseded by T972; original release-friction scan requirement is now part of the release evidence manifest scope.
tasks.description: Superseded task record. T972 owns the implementation because release-friction follow-up capture is inseparable from release evidence generation and final summary rendering.
tasks.risk: No standalone risk remains if T972 explicitly covers follow-up scan evidence and zero-follow-up rationale.

rootCauseAnalysis.findingsSummary: The final phase summary was evidence-backed for counts, but it did not force a friction/risk scan before reporting zero follow-on tasks.
rootCauseAnalysis.rootCause: The closeout summary template asks for follow-on task counts from task-store evidence, and .ai/RELEASING.md says release friction should feed improvements. There is no mandatory command or checklist step that scans the actual release transcript/tool failures and reconciles them with follow-on tasks before the final summary. Therefore '0 follow-on tasks' can be technically true from the store while operationally false from the session.
rootCauseAnalysis.recommendation: Add a pre-summary release-friction checklist or command. It should ask whether any parse failures, stale state, stashes, approval gaps, CI/release confusion, or manual recoveries occurred, and require linked follow-up tasks or an explicit 'not actionable' rationale.
rootCauseAnalysis.architecturalConsiderations: Model release evidence as an aggregate containing validation, publish proof, known risks, and linked follow-up tasks. Then render the phase summary from that aggregate rather than from task counts alone.

metadata.supersededReason: Combined with lead task after root-cause metadata review showed overlapping subsystem/root cause and a shared resolution path.

tasks.title: [CAE UX] Add guidance-set version activation, rollback, and audit controls
tasks.phase: Phase 73 — Guidance redesign and CAE management UX
tasks.approach: Make CAE version management explicit and reversible. Activating or rolling back a guidance set changes future guidance, so the UI needs deliberate controls, confirmation, and audit evidence.
technicalScope: Expose CAE registry versions with active status, created metadata, row counts, and validation status in Manage Guidance.
Add activate and rollback actions using existing CAE registry version commands and the CAE mutation approval lane.
Require confirmation, actor, and rationale before activation or rollback, and show what version will become active.
Show mutation audit results or evidence after activation/rollback and provide recovery messaging for failed validation or unavailable admin mutations.
acceptanceCriteria: Users can see active, previous, and candidate guidance-set versions without reading raw SQLite or CLI output.
Activation and rollback are impossible to trigger accidentally and require actor/rationale confirmation.
A successful activation or rollback refreshes the active guidance-set summary and shows audit/evidence details.
Failed activation or rollback leaves the current active guidance set unchanged and explains the blocker in user-facing terms.

tasks.title: [CAE UX] Build draft guidance editor and impact preview
tasks.phase: Phase 73 — Guidance redesign and CAE management UX
tasks.approach: Implement the actual editing experience for guidance changes separately from mutation plumbing. Users should be able to describe the source, trigger, strength, and acknowledgement behavior, then preview what would change before touching the active guidance set.
technicalScope: Create an edit/draft UI for guidance source text or source reference, trigger conditions, family/strength, priority, and acknowledgement requirement where supported by CAE rows.
Support starting a draft from active library rows and from prefilled Improve this guidance context.
Run preview or evaluation checks against the draft context and show before/after impact for the current task/workflow.
Keep draft editing separate from activation so users can abandon or revise changes without changing the active guidance set.
acceptanceCriteria: Users can start a draft guidance edit from either a library row or an Improve this guidance entrypoint.
The draft editor uses source, trigger, strength, and acknowledgement language instead of activation/artifact-first jargon.
Before saving or activating, users can preview the likely impact on at least the current task/workflow.
Invalid draft input is reported next to the affected field with a plain-language explanation and debug details available separately.

tasks.title: [Guidance UX] Establish visual hierarchy and first-run styling for Guidance
tasks.phase: Phase 73 — Guidance redesign and CAE management UX
tasks.approach: Give the Guidance view an intentional visual system instead of a stack of same-looking bordered cards. New users should know where to start, what changed, and what is debug-only at a glance.
technicalScope: Define stronger visual hierarchy for the primary pre-flight card, result summaries, warnings, debug details, and Manage Guidance controls.
Add first-run/empty-state styling and copy that makes the default path feel welcoming instead of dry and diagnostic.
Differentiate required guidance, recommendations, review checks, feedback, and version-management actions visually without relying only on text labels.
Keep styling aligned with VS Code/Cursor theme tokens and sidebar constraints.
acceptanceCriteria: The default Guidance view has an obvious start point and primary action before any advanced or diagnostic content.
Required, advisory, review, debug, and mutation/version-management states are visually distinguishable in light and dark themes.
Empty and first-run states avoid raw internal terminology and include a clear next action.
Renderer snapshots or tests cover key class names/sections so styling hooks do not silently disappear.

tasks.title: [Guidance UX] Throttle Guidance refresh storms and preserve user context
tasks.phase: Phase 73 — Guidance redesign and CAE management UX
tasks.approach: Stop the Guidance view from turning frequent kit SQLite or trace writes into visible UI churn. The panel should stay current without repainting history every time the engine coughs.
technicalScope: Audit GuidanceViewProvider refresh triggers from initial load, manual reload, kit state poke, acknowledgement, feedback, and preview writes.
Add debounce/throttle behavior for automatic summary refreshes without delaying user-initiated checks or explicit reloads.
Avoid clearing or moving the user's current selection, expanded details, preview result, or explanation when an unrelated background refresh occurs.
Document the expected refresh semantics for manual reload, check result, trace/history updates, and kit state changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Frequent task DB or CAE trace writes do not cause Recent Activity to visibly jump or repaint every second while the user is reading the panel.
Manual Reload status and Check Before Running still update immediately and predictably.
The selected task/workflow and open result/debug sections survive background summary refreshes where practical.
Tests or smoke coverage demonstrate throttled automatic refresh behavior and immediate user-initiated refresh behavior.

tasks.title: [Guidance UX] Add phase 73 verification coverage and manual smoke for Guidance and CAE management
tasks.phase: Phase 73 — Guidance redesign and CAE management UX
tasks.approach: Close the redesign with evidence: renderer tests for the new IA, interaction tests where practical, and a manual extension smoke path that proves the check, activity, refresh, styling, draft, version-management, and CAE mutation guardrail flows do not break each other.
technicalScope: Expand render-guidance tests for Check Before Running, Manage Guidance, Recent Activity grouping, debug details, Improve this guidance entrypoints, styling hooks, and version-management states.
Add provider or integration-level coverage for message handling, loading/focus behavior, and throttled automatic refresh where feasible without overbuilding the test harness.

Document and run a manual smoke checklist for the packaged Cursor extension covering first-run, preview, recent activity, feedback, library, draft editor, validation, activation/rollback, and disabled-admin states.
Verify existing dashboard/config extension surfaces still compile and package after Guidance changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Automated tests cover the primary rendering states introduced in phase 73, including default check flow, Manage Guidance, Recent Activity, debug details, draft editor, and version controls.
Manual smoke evidence covers both read-only and CAE update paths, including disabled admin mutations and rollback/activation guardrails.
Refresh-throttling behavior is verified so background CAE/task writes do not disrupt a user reading or editing in the Guidance view.
The extension compile/test/package commands pass or any remaining failures are documented with exact blockers.
No phase 73 Guidance change regresses the existing dashboard view or config view behavior.

tasks.title: [CAE UX] Wire CAE mutation command adapter and safety gates
tasks.phase: Phase 73 — Guidance redesign and CAE management UX
tasks.approach: Build the extension-side command adapter and guardrail layer for CAE registry mutations before building the full editor workflow. This keeps mutation approval, disabled states, actor/rationale capture, and error handling small enough to verify independently.
technicalScope: Inventory the available CAE registry admin commands needed by Manage Guidance and define a typed extension-side adapter for them.
Handle the CAE governance lane explicitly: caeMutationApproval, actor, rationale, kit.cae.enabled, registryStore sqlite, and adminMutations disabled states.
Normalize CAE mutation errors into user-facing messages that explain whether the action is unavailable, invalid, or blocked by governance.
Keep policyApproval and caeMutationApproval terminology separated so the UI does not mix approval surfaces.
acceptanceCriteria: Manage Guidance has a reusable adapter path for CAE registry admin commands instead of ad hoc calls embedded in button handlers.
When CAE admin mutations are disabled, the UI clearly shows read-only management state and does not offer live mutation buttons.
Mutation attempts require actor and rationale before sending caeMutationApproval to the CLI.
Errors from CAE registry mutation commands render as actionable user-facing messages while preserving raw details in debug output.
Tests or focused smoke coverage verify at least one allowed mutation path and one disabled/governance-blocked path.

tasks.title: [CAE UX] Add Improve this guidance entrypoints from previews and feedback
tasks.phase: Phase 73 — Guidance redesign and CAE management UX
tasks.approach: Connect the consumption path to the management path. If a card is noisy, missing, or useful, users should understand what their feedback does and be able to start a concrete CAE update draft from that context.
technicalScope: Add Improve this guidance actions to preview cards, recent activity rows, and useful/noisy feedback completion states.
Prefill update context with trace id/check record, command name, task id, guidance item id when present, source titles, and feedback signal.
Explain whether the user is proposing a change, creating a draft, or recording feedback only.
Keep existing cae-record-shadow-feedback behavior intact while adding a path into CAE draft/update UI.
acceptanceCriteria: After marking an item Noisy, the UI offers a clear next step to start a guidance update draft from that item.
Improve this guidance opens a prefilled update context that explains what will change and what remains read-only until saved/activated.
Useful feedback communicates how it helps tune guidance without implying an immediate live registry mutation.
Tests cover the rendered entrypoints and required data attributes for prefilled update context.

tasks.title: [CAE UX] Add Guidance Library and active guidance-set summary
tasks.phase: Phase 73 — Guidance redesign and CAE management UX
tasks.approach: Expose CAE as something users can understand and inspect before editing: an active guidance set with sources, triggers, strengths, validation state, and version history.
technicalScope: Add a Manage Guidance surface that summarizes the active CAE version, validation state, source count, trigger count, and last update/audit information when available.
Render a Guidance Library list from existing CAE artifact and activation read commands or dashboard summary data.
Group library entries by workflow, family/strength, phase, or source in a way that supports browsing before editing.
Use source, trigger, strength, evidence, and version terminology in the UI while keeping raw ids available in debug details.
acceptanceCriteria: Users can see which guidance set is active and whether it validates without running CLI commands manually.
Users can browse current guidance sources/triggers from the extension using plain-language labels.
The library view makes clear whether a row is required, recommended, a suggested step, or a review check.
Validation or registry-load failures show actionable recovery text and do not break the read-only pre-flight check flow.

tasks.title: [Guidance UX] Hide CAE internals behind debug details and rewrite default copy
tasks.phase: Phase 73 — Guidance redesign and CAE management UX
tasks.approach: Reduce cognitive load by replacing CAE machinery labels with product language and moving source ids, trace ids, registry details, storage, and raw JSON into explicit maintainer/debug sections.
technicalScope: Replace default labels such as Registry, Activation, Artifact, Trace, Shadow tuning, and Live evaluation with user-facing terms.
Collapse Advanced details JSON, raw preview JSON, raw trace JSON, source ids, artifact ids, storage, and active registry version by default.
Rewrite empty states and recovery copy for first-run users while preserving maintainer recovery commands in secondary text.
Ensure copy distinguishes guidance checks, guidance feedback, acknowledgements, and policy approval without repeating scary disclaimers everywhere.
acceptanceCriteria: A first-run empty state tells the user how to run their first pre-flight check in plain language.
Default preview cards explain why guidance appeared and what to do next without exposing activation/artifact ids unless expanded.
Maintainer diagnostics remain available in collapsed debug/details sections.
Tests assert the key terminology changes and that raw JSON remains copyable from debug sections.

tasks.title: [Guidance UX] Replace Recent checks firehose with deduped Recent Activity
tasks.phase: Phase 73 — Guidance redesign and CAE management UX
tasks.approach: Turn trace history from internal log spam into a useful activity summary. Repeated checks should collapse unless something meaningful changed.
technicalScope: Rename Recent checks to Recent Activity in the user-facing Guidance UI.
Group or dedupe recent traces by command, task, mode, and guidance counts or equivalent stable summary key.
Show repeat counts and last-checked timestamps for unchanged repeated checks.
Keep raw trace ids and storage details available only in a debug/details expansion.
acceptanceCriteria: Repeated identical guidance checks no longer render as a scrolling list of separate top-level rows.
The activity card communicates last check, repeated count, and whether guidance changed since the previous grouped check.
Users can still access Review why or debug trace details for a representative recent check.
Renderer tests cover empty activity, grouped repeated activity, and changed activity cases.

tasks.title: [Guidance UX] Add visible loading, completion, and focus behavior for Guidance actions
tasks.phase: Phase 73 — Guidance redesign and CAE management UX
tasks.approach: Make every button feel alive. The extension currently posts results into panels with weak feedback, so users can click a working button and still think it did fuck-all.
technicalScope: Add loading states for reload, check, explain, acknowledge, feedback, and copy actions where applicable.
Disable or mark in-progress controls while their corresponding command is running.
Scroll or focus the relevant result panel after preview, explanation, acknowledgement, or feedback output updates.
Surface concise success/failure messages near the initiating control and preserve detailed JSON behind debug drawers.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking Check Guidance immediately changes visible state before the CLI result returns.
Clicking Review why scrolls or focuses the explanation output when it arrives.
Acknowledgement and feedback actions show durable completion or cancellation feedback instead of silently returning to the summary.
Button states recover correctly after success, failure, or invalid commandArgs JSON.

tasks.title: [Guidance UX] Rebuild the default view around the pre-flight check flow
tasks.phase: Phase 73 — Guidance redesign and CAE management UX
tasks.approach: Move the current Guidance view away from diagnostics-first rendering and make the first screen answer the new-user question: what am I about to do, and what should I know before I do it?
technicalScope: Reorder GuidanceViewProvider HTML so task/workflow selection and the primary check action appear before status, history, feedback, and raw details.
Rename labels toward plain-language intent, including Before You Run or Check Before Running where appropriate.
Render preview results immediately after the check action so users see cause and effect in place.
Keep recovery/status available without letting it dominate the normal happy path.
acceptanceCriteria: Opening the Guidance view shows a clear primary pre-flight workflow before Recent Activity, acknowledgements, feedback, or raw JSON.
The primary action reads as a read-only check and does not imply that the workflow itself will run.
Preview results render adjacent to the action that produced them, not below unrelated diagnostics.
Existing tests or new renderer tests cover the new default section order and primary copy.

tasks.title: [Guidance UX] Define Before You Run and Manage Guidance information architecture
tasks.phase: Phase 73 — Guidance redesign and CAE management UX
tasks.approach: Establish the product model for Guidance as two user jobs: checking what applies before running a workflow, and managing the CAE guidance set deliberately. This creates the language, screen structure, and state model for the rest of the phase.
technicalScope: Define top-level sections or tabs for Check Before Running and Manage Guidance in the Cursor extension Guidance view.
Map CAE concepts into user-facing terms: source, trigger, strength, evidence, version, and check record.
Specify which data remains default-visible versus debug-only across status, previews, history, and CAE update flows.
Document the safety boundary between read-only guidance checks and mutating CAE guidance updates.
acceptanceCriteria: A phase 73 implementation note or code-adjacent design artifact defines the Check Before Running and Manage Guidance IA without relying on raw CAE jargon.
The IA identifies default-visible fields and debug-only fields for guidance checks, recent activity, library rows, and version state.
The plan explicitly distinguishes read-only checks from draft/save/activate CAE mutations and their required confirmations.
Follow-on extension tasks can implement from this artifact without revisiting the core product vocabulary.

tasks.title: [phase-control] Retire update-workspace-phase-snapshot as the happy-path mutator
tasks.approach: After set-current-phase exists, make update-workspace-phase-snapshot an alias, wrapper, or explicitly deprecated compatibility command. Preserve upgrade safety while ensuring new agents and operators do not start from YAML patching.
technicalScope: Decide whether update-workspace-phase-snapshot becomes an alias to set-current-phase, a thin backwards-compatible wrapper, or a deprecated command with warning output.
If kept, change behavior/documentation so SQLite authority is primary and YAML/export is compatibility only.
Update command manifest, CLI map, instruction files, response templates, and tests affected by the command rename or alias.
Preserve existing scripts/tests that call update-workspace-phase-snapshot during a compatibility window, with clear warnings or migration notes.
Add regression tests that old command usage cannot leave SQLite/config/export in a more divergent state than before.
acceptanceCriteria: New happy-path docs and CLI examples no longer teach update-workspace-phase-snapshot as the primary phase mutator.
Existing compatible invocations either route through set-current-phase or produce an actionable deprecation warning without breaking supported workflows.
YAML/export is clearly labeled non-authoritative everywhere this command is documented.
The command cannot partially update YAML and fail to update SQLite without an explicit error and repair hint.
Compatibility and migration behavior are covered by tests and release notes when appropriate.
tasks.summary: Demote the YAML-first phase snapshot command to a compatibility shim after the canonical command lands.
tasks.description: update-workspace-phase-snapshot still reads and writes compatibility YAML first, then mirrors SQLite, even though SQLite is the live authority. Keeping that as the named happy path preserves the old split-brain mental model.
tasks.risk: high

metadata.sourceFinding: Workspace phase is split across SQLite workspace status, config hints, compatibility YAML/export, and per-task phaseKey; agents overrun simple requests because the operator surface is ambiguous.

tasks.title: [CAE UX] Disambiguate Guidance from agent behavior guidance
tasks.approach: Audit user-facing extension/config copy and rename behavior-tier surfaces to Agent behavior profile where they appear near the dashboard Guidance product.
technicalScope: Find user-facing Cursor extension labels that present kit.agentGuidance as guidance near the Dashboard or Guidance tab.
Rename those surfaces to Agent behavior profile or Agent posture while preserving command names and config keys for compatibility.
Add short help text that Guidance is pre-flight workflow context, while Agent behavior controls assistant collaboration style.
Update affected renderer tests, README snippets, and machine-facing guidance where the distinction is part of the product contract.
Avoid changing CLI command names or persisted config keys unless a separate migration task is approved.
acceptanceCriteria: The Cursor UI clearly distinguishes Guidance from agent behavior/profile settings.
Users are not shown two adjacent dashboard concepts both named Guidance without explanation.
Existing CLI command names and config keys remain backwards compatible.
Documentation or help copy explains the difference in one or two sentences.
Tests or snapshots cover the renamed user-facing labels.
tasks.summary: Prevent users from confusing the Guidance tab with agent guidance/behavior profile settings.
tasks.description: The product has CAE Guidance in the extension and kit.agentGuidance / resolve-agent-guidance in configuration. Both can surface as guidance language, which is a recipe for user confusion.
tasks.risk: medium

metadata.sourceFinding: Guidance already has the core CAE capabilities, but the UI still asks users to understand internal CAE concepts before the happy path is obvious.

tasks.title: [CAE UX] Make raw JSON truncation explicit and copyable
tasks.approach: Centralize raw JSON rendering with truncation notices and a route to copy or open the full payload when practical.
technicalScope: Create a helper for raw JSON blocks in render-guidance.ts that records whether content was truncated and shows the display limit.
Use the helper for Advanced details, Raw result, and Raw trace JSON blocks.
Add a copy-full-json or open-full-json affordance through the webview provider when the full payload is available in memory.
If full-copy support is deferred, show an explicit truncated notice and the command users can run to retrieve the full payload.
Add tests for truncated and non-truncated payload rendering.
acceptanceCriteria: Every truncated raw JSON block visibly says it is truncated and names the displayed limit.
Non-truncated raw JSON blocks do not show misleading truncation copy.
Users have an obvious path to retrieve or copy the full payload for debugging.
The helper is reused across summary, preview, trace detail, and action result raw blocks.
Tests prove large payloads cannot silently render as apparently complete JSON.
tasks.summary: Stop raw payload previews from pretending truncated JSON is complete.
tasks.description: Raw result/details blocks slice JSON to fixed byte-ish limits. Without a truncation notice, users may assume the displayed payload is complete.
tasks.risk: low

metadata.sourceFinding: Guidance already has the core CAE capabilities, but the UI still asks users to understand internal CAE concepts before the happy path is obvious.

tasks.title: [CAE UX] Make Guidance recovery cards plain-language and role-aware
tasks.approach: Split recovery content into user-facing diagnosis plus advanced maintainer commands/details.
technicalScope: Rewrite recovery cards for disabled Guidance, invalid registry, persistence disabled, and SQLite unavailable using plain-language headings and next actions.
Move exact CLI commands, raw codes, and storage details into Advanced details within each recovery card.
Differentiate recoverable preview-only states from hard blockers that prevent guidance cards from being trustworthy.
Keep command names accurate for maintainers and do not remove existing recovery paths.
Add renderer tests for each degraded state and for command/code placement in Advanced details.
acceptanceCriteria: A non-expert user can understand what is wrong and whether previews still work from the recovery card alone.
Maintainers still have exact CLI commands and error codes available without raw JSON spelunking.
Disabled Guidance, invalid registry, persistence disabled, and unavailable SQLite each have distinct recovery copy.
Hard blockers are visually distinct from history-only degradation.
Tests cover all recovery-card variants.
tasks.summary: Help users recover from degraded Guidance without parsing operator commands first.
tasks.description: Recovery cards currently surface commands and storage details quickly. That is helpful for maintainers, but regular dashboard users need a simple diagnosis and next action first.
tasks.risk: medium

metadata.sourceFinding: Guidance already has the core CAE capabilities, but the UI still asks users to understand internal CAE concepts before the happy path is obvious.

tasks.title: [CAE UX] Make read acknowledgements title-first and understandable
tasks.approach: Enrich pending acknowledgement rendering with source titles and use Mark as read copy while keeping the command payload unchanged.
technicalScope: Update pending acknowledgement rendering to show guidance title/source title before activationId.
Use Mark as read as the primary button label and keep activationId/ackToken in hidden attributes or details.
Add explanatory copy that separates read acknowledgement from sensitive-command permission.
Improve acknowledgement action result copy to say Read guidance recorded or Mark as read failed.
Add tests for title-first rendering, fallback activation-id rendering, and action copy.
acceptanceCriteria: Users can tell what they are marking as read without reading an activation id.
Mark as read actions still call cae-satisfy-ack with the correct traceId, activationId, and ackToken.
The UI continues to state that marking guidance as read is not policy approval for another command.
Fallback rows remain safe when source titles are unavailable.
Tests assert the button label, title-first display, and hidden technical identifiers.
tasks.summary: Turn acknowledgement rows into human read receipts instead of activation-id actions.
tasks.description: Pending acknowledgement rows expose activation ids and acknowledgement strength. Users need to know what guidance they are marking as read and why it matters.
tasks.risk: medium

metadata.sourceFinding: Guidance already has the core CAE capabilities, but the UI still asks users to understand internal CAE concepts before the happy path is obvious.

tasks.title: [CAE UX] Open Why this appeared inline with the relevant item
tasks.approach: Change explain rendering to target an inline container associated with the clicked card or recent check while preserving a fallback global detail area.
technicalScope: Add stable per-card/per-row explain containers or identifiers in render-guidance output.
Update GuidanceViewProvider message handling so explain results can be inserted beside the clicked guidance card or recent check.
Show inline loading, success, and failure states for Why this appeared.
Keep raw trace JSON collapsed under Advanced details within the inline explanation.
Add provider/bootstrap tests or renderer coverage for inline explain target behavior.

The explanation includes the human summary before raw JSON.
The user can open explanations for multiple items without losing the preview result context.

tasks.title: [CAE UX] Make recent Guidance checks human-readable
tasks.approach: Render recent trace summaries around persisted task/workflow metadata and keep trace ids copyable in details.
technicalScope: Update renderTraceRows to prefer taskId, taskTitle, commandName, createdAt, mode, family counts, pending acknowledgement count, and conflict count from recent trace summaries.
Move traceId into a details element or secondary copy affordance instead of making it the row title.
Add clear fallback behavior for older trace rows without persisted summary metadata.
Consider including relative/friendly time when feasible without adding brittle date dependencies.

Family counts, pending read count, and conflict count are visible when present.

tasks.title: [CAE UX] Add decision-oriented preview summaries
tasks.approach: Add a summary band at the top of preview results that converts family counts, acknowledgements, conflicts, and mode into a short decision aid.
technicalScope: Update renderGuidancePreviewInnerHtml to generate a plain-language result summary from familyCounts, pendingAcknowledgements, conflictShadowSummary, task, command, and evalMode.
Add a positive empty state for zero guidance: no special guidance found, you can continue.
Make required rules, pending mark-as-read items, and conflicts visually outrank advisory suggestions.
Preserve grouped Rules/Consider/Steps/Review sections below the summary for detailed reading.
Add renderer tests for no guidance, advisory-only guidance, required rules, pending read items, and conflicts.

Required rules, pending read actions, and conflicts are called out before advisory guidance.

Preview mode copy consistently says would apply; live evaluation mode uses distinct copy without implying the workflow ran.

tasks.title: [CAE UX] Normalize Guidance labels and remove internal CAE jargon
tasks.approach: Perform a focused copy pass over Guidance UI strings, preserving technical terms only in Advanced details or raw JSON.
technicalScope: Rename Shadow tuning to Guidance feedback in summary cards and confirmation copy.
Rename Noisy feedback to Not relevant while preserving the stored signal value noisy.
Rename Acknowledge/Acknowledgement in user actions to Mark as read / Read guidance where possible.
Rename Trace detail UI headings to Why this appeared while preserving trace terminology in Advanced details.
Update renderer tests and any machine-facing plan copy that describes the intended user labels.

Internal terms CAE, activation, artifact, trace, shadow, and policyApproval appear only where needed for Advanced details, command output, or explicit disambiguation.
Feedback buttons read Useful and Not relevant while persisted payloads still use useful/noisy as required by the command contract.
Acknowledgement copy still clearly says marking guidance as read is not permission to run a sensitive command.

tasks.description: Several labels still expose internal terms such as Shadow tuning, Noisy, Trace detail, and Acknowledgement. These are accurate to CAE, but confusing in a user-facing dashboard.

tasks.title: [CAE UX] Replace command-first workflow selection with intent labels
tasks.approach: Add a curated intent catalog for Guidance that maps friendly labels to command names while keeping manifest-backed search for advanced coverage.
technicalScope: Introduce friendly curated workflow labels and descriptions for the existing CURATED_WORKFLOW_NAMES set.
