tasks.title: [phase-control] Reconcile phase authority docs and command instructions
tasks.approach: Audit the machine-facing phase docs and instruction markdown, then align them to the implemented precedence and the new operator commands. Keep human docs out of routine agent bootstrap unless generated or explicitly touched.
technicalScope: Update src/modules/task-engine/instructions/queue-health.md to match implementation: workspace status wins, config is fallback only when no workspace status phase exists, doctor notes config drift instead of failing solely for mismatch.

Update update-workspace-phase-snapshot, update-workspace-status, agent-session-snapshot, AGENT-CLI-MAP, WORKSPACE-KIT-SESSION, and related .ai runbooks to point at set-current-phase and phase-status as the happy path once implemented.
Remove wording that makes docs/maintainers/data/workspace-kit-status.yaml sound authoritative for runtime readers.
Add or update docs/check coverage so instruction text for phase precedence does not regress against phase-resolution.ts behavior.
Include migration notes for T836/T546/T547 history so old completed/wishlist rows do not mislead future agents.
acceptanceCriteria: Machine-facing instructions agree that kit_workspace_status is canonical when available and kit.currentPhaseNumber is only a bootstrap/UX hint.
No agent-facing instruction says doctor fails solely because config currentPhaseNumber disagrees with SQLite workspace status.
The recommended command for reading phase state is phase-status; the recommended command for mutating it is set-current-phase.
The relationship between workspace current phase and task phaseKey is stated consistently in all touched guidance.
Docs/check or targeted tests fail if queue-health instruction precedence drifts away from implementation again.
tasks.summary: Remove contradictory agent-facing phase guidance so agents stop choosing the wrong source of truth.
tasks.description: The canon says SQLite kit_workspace_status wins and config is only a UX hint, but some instruction text still says queue-health prefers config or doctor fails on phase mismatch. This contradiction causes agents to perform redundant and sometimes stale repairs.
tasks.risk: medium

metadata.sourceFinding: Workspace phase is split across SQLite workspace status, config hints, compatibility YAML/export, and per-task phaseKey; agents overrun simple requests because the operator surface is ambiguous.

tasks.title: [phase-control] Split next-phase task creation from phase movement
tasks.approach: Add a narrow command or documented command pattern for creating proposed or ready tasks from a plan for a target phase, and update agent guidance so set-current-phase never creates tasks as a side effect.
technicalScope: Define the supported operator flow for “open tasks for next phase” as separate from “set current phase”.
Add or refine a command surface such as create-phase-tasks-from-plan / persist planning execution drafts for targetPhaseKey with explicit desired status proposed|ready.
Require an explicit planRef and targetPhaseKey for generated tasks; do not infer target phase from workspace current phase unless the user asks for that default.
Update agent-facing guidance to avoid invoking task-to-phase-branch, release closeout, or run-transition loops for simple phase/task backlog setup.
Add tests or golden CLI examples showing phase movement alone creates zero tasks, while next-phase task creation creates only the requested proposed/ready rows.
acceptanceCriteria: A request to set current phase can be completed without creating, accepting, starting, or completing any task rows.
A request to open tasks for a target phase has an explicit task-creation path with targetPhaseKey, status, planRef, and idempotency keys.
Generated next-phase tasks include real development fields: summary, description, approach, technicalScope, acceptanceCriteria, risk, phase, and phaseKey.
Agent guidance tells agents to ask before creating tasks when the user only requested phase movement.
Regression coverage or fixtures prove no phase-status/set-current-phase command mutates task rows.
tasks.summary: Prevent phase rollover requests from automatically expanding into planning, task creation, or delivery workflows.
tasks.description: The current agent guidance lets “put us at phase N” blur into “create/open tasks for phase N” and sometimes into closeout or delivery playbooks. Define a separate next-phase task creation workflow so phase movement stays small unless the user explicitly asks for backlog creation.
tasks.risk: medium

metadata.sourceFinding: Workspace phase is split across SQLite workspace status, config hints, compatibility YAML/export, and per-task phaseKey; agents overrun simple requests because the operator surface is ambiguous.

tasks.title: [phase-control] Add read-only phase-status command
tasks.approach: Build a read-only command over the existing workspace status reader, config resolver, and task phase bucketing helpers. Keep it fast, structured, and independent from heavyweight planning or closeout workflows.
technicalScope: Add workspace-kit run phase-status with optional includeTaskCounts and includeDriftDetails flags.
Return canonical current/next phase from kit_workspace_status, config hint/label values, configMatchesWorkspaceStatus, export freshness when cheap to compute, and clear drift remediation suggestions.
Return current-phase and next-phase task counts grouped by status when requested, using task.phaseKey / inferred phase only for task buckets.
Avoid mutating state and avoid requiring agents to parse docs, config files, YAML, or raw SQLite directly.
Add instruction markdown, manifest row, CLI map examples, and tests for aligned state, config drift, missing workspace status row, and no phase configured.
acceptanceCriteria: phase-status answers canonical current phase and next phase in one JSON payload.
phase-status explicitly distinguishes workspace phase from per-task phaseKey buckets.
phase-status can show open/proposed/ready counts for current and next phase without changing the ready queue.
When config or export drift exists, the payload names the exact command to repair it instead of making doctor output the only clue.
The command is Tier C/read-only and works as the recommended first response to phase-discovery chat prompts.
tasks.summary: Give agents and operators one cheap read-only answer for current phase, next phase, drift, and phase task counts.
tasks.description: Simple questions like “what phase are we in?” and “what tasks are open for next phase?” currently push agents toward doctor, queue-health, dashboard-summary, list-tasks, config inspection, and sometimes raw SQLite. Add a focused phase-status command that reports canonical phase state and actionable drift without doing work.
tasks.risk: medium

metadata.sourceFinding: Workspace phase is split across SQLite workspace status, config hints, compatibility YAML/export, and per-task phaseKey; agents overrun simple requests because the operator surface is ambiguous.

tasks.title: [phase-control] Add canonical set-current-phase command
tasks.approach: Implement a new task-engine command that updates the SQLite workspace status as the live authority, derives or updates remaining compatibility hints in a clearly documented order, and returns before/after plus drift verification. Treat update-workspace-phase-snapshot as a compatibility path, not the primary operator entry point.
technicalScope: Add a workspace-kit run set-current-phase command with JSON args for currentKitPhase, optional nextKitPhase, optional label/focus text, actor, dryRun, and idempotency key.
Make SQLite kit_workspace_status the first write and live authority; update config hint/label only as compatibility or UX fields, never as canonical truth.
Return a concise verification payload covering canonical phase, next phase, config hint alignment, export status, workspace revision, and suggested follow-up command when drift remains.
Support dryRun with the exact before/after plan and no writes so agents can preview the phase rollover safely.
Add command manifest, instruction markdown, AGENT-CLI-MAP entry, and tests for success, dry run, stale revision/idempotency, and validation failures.
acceptanceCriteria: A single CLI invocation can set current phase and next phase without manually running update-workspace-phase-snapshot, update-workspace-status, config set, and export commands in sequence.
The command is non-interactive, retry-safe, and emits machine-readable before/after verification data suitable for agents and the Cursor extension.
Doctor passes after the happy path without config-vs-SQLite phase drift caused by the command.
Existing workspace status revision semantics remain intact and no task phaseKey values are modified by this workspace-level command.
Tests prove dryRun performs no writes and repeated idempotent invocations do not duplicate audit events.
tasks.summary: Create one blessed operator command for changing the workspace current phase without split-brain state.
tasks.description: Operators currently have to coordinate SQLite workspace status, config phase hints, optional YAML/export surfaces, and doctor notes. Add a single non-interactive set-current-phase command that owns the happy path and produces verification evidence instead of making agents stitch together several mutators.
tasks.risk: high

tasks.title: Add explicit plan mode with editable, approvable execution plans

metadata.problemStatement:
Concept: Before mutating code, produce a structured plan artifact reviewable by human or supervisor agent—editable and approvable. Maps to Claude Code plan review and WC governance.

Why this matters: Bridges strict process and fast agentic execution.
metadata.expectedOutcome: Plan artifact linked to tasks with sections: assumptions; intended files; commands to run; risks; rollback strategy; required approvals. Commands: draft-plan, revise-plan, approve-plan, execute-plan. Cursor can render/edit; Workflow Cannon owns schema and approval semantics.
metadata.impact: Reduces unapproved large diffs; improves evidence for why changes occurred.
metadata.constraints:
Suggested architecture: Versioned plan JSON/Markdown hybrid; approval gate blocks execute-plan until signed; integrate with hooks and task transitions.

Likely dependencies: Policy approval patterns; planning module overlap—merge or extend deliberately.

Decomposition notes: Reuse planning module concepts where possible; avoid duplicate build-plan confusion—name and scope clearly.
metadata.successSignals: Approved plan required for sensitive execute path; revision history; execute-plan emits evidence bundle; docs show end-to-end flow.
metadata.evidenceRef: docs/maintainers/runbooks/wishlist-workflow.md; maintainer wishlist batch 2026-04-02

tasks.title: Add asynchronous remote execution with Cursor background-agent handoff

metadata.problemStatement:
Concept: Cursor background agents run async on isolated remotes, clone repos, run commands, accept follow-ups. Workflow Cannon should add a control plane treating them as managed workers, not standalone UI.

Why this matters: Scale and long-running autonomy without rebuilding remote compute.
metadata.expectedOutcome: Remote-run adapter: launch background agent from a task; persist remote run metadata; sync results/evidence back; support clean take-over-in-Cursor handoff. Kit owns launch criteria, approval gates, task linkage, evidence expectations, completion semantics. Cursor owns remote machine and editor environment.
metadata.impact: Long-running work stays tied to task engine state and approvals.
metadata.constraints:
Suggested architecture: Remote run records in SQLite; state machine (queued/running/needs-input/completed/failed); idempotent launch.

Likely dependencies: Cursor APIs or manual bridge documented; team orchestration may share assignment model.

Decomposition notes: MVP = metadata + human sync; automate sync when APIs exist; explicit failure paths when remote diverges.
metadata.successSignals: list-remote-runs (or equivalent) shows linkage to T###; evidence artifacts attached on completion; runbook for handoff.
metadata.evidenceRef: docs/maintainers/runbooks/wishlist-workflow.md; maintainer wishlist batch 2026-04-02

tasks.title: Add a first-class project memory layer for agent recall and continuity

metadata.problemStatement:
Concept: Strong documentation canon exists, but an explicit memory layer is needed for recurring operational knowledge, preferred patterns, environment assumptions, and learned behaviors. Claude Code uses CLAUDE.md plus auto memory; Workflow Cannon should offer a governed equivalent.

Why this matters: Docs are not memory—memory should optimize for recall and operational reuse.
metadata.expectedOutcome: Separate memory surface (e.g. CANNON.md plus structured memory records in persistence). Categories: build/runtime commands; architecture conventions; debugging discoveries; repo-specific gotchas; review preferences. Commands: list-memory, write-memory, approve-memory, prune-memory, explain-memory-precedence. Explicitly not mixed with maintainer canon or generated docs.
metadata.impact: Improves session continuity and reduces repeated rediscovery without polluting canonical docs.
metadata.constraints:
Suggested architecture: Distinct store or namespaced rows; approval workflow for promoted memory; precedence explainer ties config + memory + canon.

Likely dependencies: Policy for write-memory; task linkage optional; migration story from ad-hoc notes.

Decomposition notes: Start with human-curated memory only; defer fuzzy retrieval/ML; document boundary vs .ai/ and docs/maintainers/.
metadata.successSignals: Precedence command returns deterministic merge story; approved vs draft memory states; prune is audited; no collision with document-project outputs.
metadata.evidenceRef: docs/maintainers/runbooks/wishlist-workflow.md; maintainer wishlist batch 2026-04-02

- **Ready** means triage accepted the *spec/plan* for implementation—not that CAE is shipped.
- Kit ops: pass **`expectedPlanningGeneration`** on **`update-task`** / **`run-transition`** when policy requires it.
- **`run-transition` `accept`** is Tier A: JSON **`policyApproval`** on the argv object (see `.ai/POLICY-APPROVAL.md`, `.ai/AGENT-CLI-MAP.md`).
tasks.risk: medium

reviewNotes: ADR: default `.ai/adrs/` for machine canon; cross-link maintainer ADR only if required.
Document CAE vs `ModuleActivationReport` naming collision in ADR table.

tasks.title: Planning: multi-task decomposition from build-plan

metadata.problemStatement: Primary durable handoff is wishlist intake; outputMode tasks persists a single proposed T###. Teams still manually decompose via convert-wishlist or follow-up work. There is no first-class path from one interview to multiple execution tasks with provenance.
metadata.expectedOutcome: After finalize, operators can generate a vetted set of execution tasks (or a structured decomposition payload) from the planning artifact in one kit-supported flow, with stable metadata.planningProvenance-style links.
metadata.impact: Closes the gap between rich planning answers and actionable queue items without repetitive manual splitting.
metadata.constraints: Must align with convert-wishlist decomposition schema and strict task validation; respect planning-generation policy on bulk creates; optional feature flag or outputMode extension.
metadata.successSignals: Instruction + tests for new path; example JSON in AGENT-CLI-MAP or planning instructions; at least one integration test.
metadata.evidenceRef: chat:planning-module-ux-gaps 2026-04-07

wishlistConversionDecomposition.rationale: Separate planning finalize contract (multi-task or decomposition envelope) from task-engine bulk persistence under planningGeneration, then ship operator docs and an integration test so the path is discoverable and regression-safe.
wishlistConversionDecomposition.boundaries: Does not implement the Cursor extension interview UI (T800/T801); does not change Tier A/B policy lanes; optional feature flag only if needed to stage behavior.
wishlistConversionDecomposition.dependencyIntent: T810 applies and validates payloads produced or stabilized by T809; T811 documents and tests against the shipped CLI/planning behavior.

tasks.title: Planning: first-class interview UI in Cursor extension

metadata.problemStatement: Operators run planning as JSON in a terminal or via agent chat. The dashboard Planning card only shows progress percent and a resume CLI line—no in-question UI, no structured form for build-plan answers.
metadata.expectedOutcome: Users can complete a guided planning interview from the Workflow Cannon dashboard (or dedicated webview) with one question at a time, validated fields, and a clear path to finalize without hand-assembling JSON.
metadata.impact: Lowers friction for humans who do not want to drive workspace-kit JSON manually; reduces agent errors on build-plan payloads.
metadata.constraints: Must stay consistent with dashboard-summary planningSession contract; mutations still go through workspace-kit run (extension does not write SQLite directly); obey tasks.planningGenerationPolicy on any new mutators.
metadata.successSignals: Extension ships a planning flow that invokes or mirrors build-plan semantics; E2E or integration test covers at least one planningType end-to-end from UI.
metadata.evidenceRef: chat:planning-module-ux-gaps 2026-04-07

wishlistConversionDecomposition.rationale: Ship the guided interview as implementable UI work first, then lock it with an automated end-to-end proof so planningSession and build-plan semantics do not regress.
wishlistConversionDecomposition.boundaries: Extension and test harness only; no direct task-engine SQLite writes from the UI; planning question definitions stay in the planning module unless an adapter shim is unavoidable.
wishlistConversionDecomposition.dependencyIntent: T801 depends on T800 landing the interview surface and host bridge.

Optional: dedupe policy copy if another surface already covers POLICY-APPROVAL
acceptanceCriteria: No dead static-only approvals card in production, or queue data matches `pnpm exec wk run list-approval-queue` for the same workspace
Extension README / fixture tests updated if HTML contract changes

tasks.title: [kit+extension] Refresh effective agent-behavior Cursor rule when config or store changes
tasks.approach: After T782 lands, invoke the sync command automatically when role or temperament inputs change: post-success in workspace-kit mutators (set-agent-guidance, set-active-behavior-profile, create/update/delete-behavior-profile, interview-behavior-profile finalize with apply) and debounced from the Cursor extension StateWatcher alongside existing dashboard refresh (config.json and task SQLite already watched). Fail open: sync errors log or return advisory warning without blocking primary command.
technicalScope: Shared internal helper callable from module onCommand handlers after persistence succeeds; avoid circular imports.
Extension: debounced subprocess or reuse existing wk invocation path used for dashboard-summary; document any new env or setting.
Optional kit.lifecycleHooks after-module-command example in .ai for operators who disable extension.
Update onboarding or collaboration playbooks in .ai to state that the generated rule refreshes after saves.
acceptanceCriteria: Changing kit.agentGuidance tier or active behavior profile updates the generated rule without a manual sync step in normal flows.
External edits to config or SQLite still refresh within debounce window when the extension is active.
Primary mutations still succeed if sync fails; behavior is covered by test or documented manual check.

tasks.title: [kit] Render effective role × temperament as Cursor rule (sync command)
tasks.approach: Add a workspace-kit run command that reads merged effective config plus agent-behavior store (same inputs as resolve-behavior-profile), renders a short Cursor rule markdown body with alwaysApply, and writes the default path under .cursor/rules (overridable via args with workspace-relative allowlist). Reuse resolveAgentGuidanceFromEffectiveConfig for role (catalog description plus hints), BehaviorProfileStore effective profile plus advisoryModulationForProfile; do not duplicate inference logic.
technicalScope: Pure renderer module plus atomic file write; YAML frontmatter for alwaysApply true; footer comment with content hash or schemaVersion.
Register command on agent-behavior module (or split if policy prefers workspace-config); confirm Tier C in policy manifest for writes under .cursor only.
Golden or snapshot tests for a few tier times profile combinations.
Document in .ai/AGENT-CLI-MAP.md Tier C with copy-paste JSON; short .ai/runbook optional for git commit versus gitignore of generated rule.
acceptanceCriteria: New sync command produces deterministic markdown from current kit state and returns JSON including output path and version or hash.
Rendered rule includes role line, temperament summary, advisory modulation as imperative bullets, and explicit advisory-only subordination to PRINCIPLES and policy.
Tests cover renderer; pnpm run wk doctor passes.

tasks.title: [extension] Dashboard New Plan: guided planning interview
tasks.approach: Implement dashboard entry (e.g. New Plan) that walks operators through planning type selection and iterative build-plan steps via the same JSON/contracts the CLI uses; wire finalize path to create wishlist intake consistent with create-wishlist or equivalent kit command; handle planning-generation policy by threading expectedPlanningGeneration from kit reads.
technicalScope: Discover current planning module commands and extension dashboard integration points (cursor-workflow-cannon).
Build interview UI state machine: prompts, validation, error surfacing from kit stderr/JSON.
Implement finalize → wishlist persistence path; verify list-wishlist shows new intake with coherent fields.
Add regression path: same inputs via CLI produce equivalent persisted shape (document or automated check).
acceptanceCriteria: Operator can complete New Plan from dashboard without hand-authored CLI JSON.
Flow invokes list-planning-types / build-plan semantics (or shared helper) — not a forked planner.
Successful finalize creates a wishlist row discoverable via wk run list-wishlist.
Policy-sensitive steps pass expectedPlanningGeneration when store policy is require.

tasks.title: [extension/docs] Surface collaboration profiles: slash hub and wk agent-behavior discovery
tasks.approach: Add or extend dashboard and README entry points linking to /collaboration-profiles, /onboarding, /behavior-interview and the matching pnpm exec wk run agent-behavior commands; state clearly that chat cannot replace JSON policyApproval.
technicalScope: Audit `.cursor/commands/collaboration-profiles.md` and agent-behavior module instructions; align copy.
Add dashboard card or section plus README pointer with one mental model for slash vs CLI.
Document advisory-only nature and policy gate disclaimer per wishlist constraints.
acceptanceCriteria: README or dashboard surfaces /collaboration-profiles and related slash entry points.
Operators can discover matching wk run agent-behavior commands from the same surface.
Explicit note that chat does not satisfy policyApproval for gated workspace-kit run commands.

tasks.title: Dashboard New Plan: guided planning interview

metadata.problemStatement: Operators need a first-class dashboard path into the planning module so they do not have to memorize CLI JSON; the interview should mirror kit semantics and produce the same artifacts.
metadata.expectedOutcome: One-click New Plan runs list-planning-types / build-plan iteratively; finalize can persist W### for wishlist triage.
metadata.impact: Improves discoverability of planning workflows and reduces friction between extension UI and workspace-kit state.
metadata.constraints: Must stay aligned with AGENT-CLI-MAP and policy gates (expectedPlanningGeneration when required); no duplicate planning logic in the extension.
metadata.successSignals: Operator completes interview from dashboard; wishlist shows new W### with coherent title and problem statement; CLI replay yields same shape.
metadata.evidenceRef: planning:new-feature:2026-04-06T18:38:11.102Z

goals: Let operators start and finish guided planning interviews from the Workflow Cannon dashboard, with visible next questions and a clean handoff to a persisted wishlist artifact when they choose.
artifact.approach: Context-driven workflow guided by planning rules.
majorTechnicalDecisions: Dashboard triggers the same CLI-shaped interview the kit already implements so behavior stays single-sourced; extension only orchestrates prompts and displays kit output.
risksAndConstraints: Constraint: Must stay aligned with AGENT-CLI-MAP and policy gates (expectedPlanningGeneration when required); no duplicate planning logic in the extension.

sourceAnswers.featureGoal: Let operators start and finish guided planning interviews from the Workflow Cannon dashboard, with visible next questions and a clean handoff to a persisted wishlist artifact when they choose.
sourceAnswers.placement: Cursor / VS Code extension dashboard panel (Workflow Cannon webview)
sourceAnswers.technology: TypeScript in the extension host; reuse existing workspace-kit run JSON contracts (build-plan, list-planning-types); no new native dependencies.
sourceAnswers.targetAudience: Maintainers and AI agent operators who drive workspace-kit from the editor.
sourceAnswers.decisionRationale: Dashboard triggers the same CLI-shaped interview the kit already implements so behavior stays single-sourced; extension only orchestrates prompts and displays kit output.
sourceAnswers.problemStatement: Operators need a first-class dashboard path into the planning module so they do not have to memorize CLI JSON; the interview should mirror kit semantics and produce the same artifacts.
sourceAnswers.expectedOutcome: One-click New Plan runs list-planning-types / build-plan iteratively; finalize can persist W### for wishlist triage.

wishlistConversionDecomposition.rationale: Vertical slice: dashboard-driven guided interview that drives the same planning module flows as CLI (list-planning-types, build-plan iterations) and can finalize into wishlist intake without reimplementing kit policy in the extension.
wishlistConversionDecomposition.boundaries: No duplicate planning business logic in extension—call workspace-kit / shared contracts; respect expectedPlanningGeneration and AGENT-CLI-MAP; MVP may use subprocess or extension bridge already used elsewhere.
wishlistConversionDecomposition.dependencyIntent: Standalone from T780; optional ordering with extension work but no hard dependsOn.

tasks.title: Collaboration profiles: slash + CLI discoverability

metadata.problemStatement: Agent-behavior and chat playbooks are easy to miss next to task-engine and dashboard flows.
metadata.expectedOutcome: Users discover Cursor slash /collaboration-profiles (hub), /onboarding, /behavior-interview, and matching wk run agent-behavior commands from one mental model.
metadata.impact: Better default agent sessions without implying chat overrides policyApproval JSON.
metadata.constraints: Advisory profiles only; document that chat cannot substitute policy gates.
metadata.successSignals: Dashboard or README surfaces /collaboration-profiles; fewer "how do I set temperament?" questions.
metadata.evidenceRef: Feature breakdown item 12; .cursor/commands/collaboration-profiles.md

wishlistConversionDecomposition.rationale: Single slice: surface existing collaboration-profile slash commands and agent-behavior CLI from dashboard/README so operators find them without spelunking.
wishlistConversionDecomposition.boundaries: No policy tier or policyApproval semantics changes; advisory profiles only; no new agent-behavior engines beyond discoverability.

tasks.title: Approvals queue discoverability: CLI and agent canon

.ai/AGENT-CLI-MAP.md and .ai/POLICY-APPROVAL.md cross-links

Optional: dashboard-summary or existing task-engine commands that expose approvals context

JSON policyApproval requirement for gated runs remains explicit in updated docs
No regression to approvals persistence contracts or review-item semantics

tasks.title: Human approvals queue — operator discoverability

metadata.problemStatement: Sensitive workflow decisions need clear human sign-off; operators should find pending approvals without spelunking the approvals module or ad-hoc CLI memory.
metadata.expectedOutcome: Documented paths: list/review approvals via wk run and/or the extension; aligned with .ai/POLICY-APPROVAL.md and existing approvals contracts.
metadata.impact: Lowers governance mistakes and makes policy gates legible for teams shipping with Workspace Kit.
metadata.constraints: No weakening of JSON policyApproval for gated commands; stay compatible with existing approvals SQLite shapes.
metadata.successSignals: README or dashboard links to approval commands; operators report they can answer "what is waiting on me?" quickly.
metadata.evidenceRef: Feature breakdown item 8 (human approvals); src/modules/approvals

wishlistConversionDecomposition.rationale: Split canon and CLI clarity from extension UX so operators get documented wk paths first, then dashboard affordances, without changing policyApproval semantics or approvals storage shapes.
wishlistConversionDecomposition.boundaries: No weakening of JSON policyApproval on gated runs; no SQLite schema breaks for approvals or policy traces; ML or fuzzy retrieval out of scope.
wishlistConversionDecomposition.dependencyIntent: T755 (docs plus CLI inventory and any read-only list or inspect path) completes before T756 (dashboard or extension surfacing) so UI links match shipped command contracts.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Wire optional auto-checkpoints before high-risk mutating operations
tasks.approach: After manual checkpoint commands ship, add opt-in hooks or orchestration points that call create-checkpoint before multi-file writes, dependency upgrades, generated refactors, PR automation, or policy-sensitive run transitions as configured per workspace.
technicalScope: Configuration surface for enablement and which risk classes trigger auto-checkpoint
Integration points in existing hook or command pipelines without blocking unrelated flows
Tests using dry-run or fixture triggers where feasible
acceptanceCriteria: Auto-checkpoint can be enabled and exercises at least one high-risk path in tests or documented pilot
Default remains opt-in per wishlist decomposition notes
Failure to checkpoint surfaces explicit errors rather than silent skip when enabled

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Implement rewind-to-checkpoint with safety guardrails and tests
tasks.approach: Add rewind command that restores working tree from a recorded ref, fail-closed on ambiguous or unsafe states, and document operator recovery. Never silently destroy reflog; require explicit flags or separate maintenance docs if destructive git operations are out of scope.
technicalScope: rewind-to-checkpoint command with validation against recorded manifest
Tests that assert predictable tree state after rewind in fixture repo
Runbook notes for submodule and vendor limitations
acceptanceCriteria: Rewind restores working tree predictably in tested scenarios
Compare plus rewind workflow is covered by automated tests
Safety behavior is documented when rewind must refuse

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Implement create-checkpoint, list-checkpoints, and compare-checkpoint commands
tasks.approach: Implement module commands per the approved model: persist checkpoint rows, capture git refs and manifests, expose JSON CLI output suitable for agents, and add compare summarizing tree diff versus checkpoint.
technicalScope: Instruction files and router registration for the three commands
Unit tests for happy paths and at least one dirty-workspace edge case
Documentation of JSON args in machine canon paths only where the module already publishes patterns
acceptanceCriteria: workspace-kit run can create, list, and compare checkpoints with stable JSON contracts
Tests cover stash and commit modes described in T748 design

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Define checkpoint record model and persistence for task-linked rewind
tasks.approach: Produce a merged design artifact under .ai (ADR or runbook section): checkpoint entity fields (git ref kind and name, changed-file manifest, task id, agent or actor id, action type, timestamps), persistence mapping in kit stores, opt-in defaults, and explicit warnings for submodules and vendor directories.
technicalScope: TypeScript-oriented field list compatible with future task-engine or module persistence
Stash versus commit-pointer modes with selection rules
Cross-links to policy approval and hook surfaces without changing policy in this task
acceptanceCriteria: Design is merged and names storage location and migration posture
Submodule and vendor caveats are explicit; no silent reflog destruction assumed

tasks.title: Design Cursor 3 task-flow subagent packaging model

Produce a short design in .ai (ADR or runbook section): invocation pattern, which rules or skills attach to the subagent versus the main session, and explicit non-goals (policy remains JSON on run; kit remains source of truth).
technicalScope: Product capability matrix versus wishlist success signals
File layout proposal under repo conventions (.cursor, .claude/skills, playbooks)
Handoff checklist: queue reads, run-transition, branch and PR loop references
acceptanceCriteria: Merged design artifact names packaging approach and Cursor version assumptions
Checklist links to existing canon paths; no claim that chat substitutes policyApproval

tasks.title: Add automatic checkpoints and task-linked rewind for safe autonomous execution

metadata.problemStatement:
Concept: Capture cheap rollback points before major mutating steps. Claude Code checkpointing lowers the trust barrier for autonomous edits.

Why this matters: More autonomy requires more reversibility.
metadata.expectedOutcome: Checkpoint subsystem recording: git commit or stash refs; changed files; task ID; agent ID; action type; timestamp. Commands: create-checkpoint, list-checkpoints, rewind-to-checkpoint, compare-checkpoint. Auto checkpoints before: multi-file writes, dependency upgrades, generated refactors, PR creation, risky task transitions.
metadata.impact: Operators can experiment with agentic batches with a governed safety net.
metadata.constraints:
Suggested architecture: Store pointers + file manifest; integrate with git workspace assumptions; never destroy reflog silently.

Likely dependencies: Hooks (before-write/before-pr); policy for rewind; clear docs when submodules/vendor dirs involved.

Decomposition notes: Opt-in per workspace first; default auto-checkpoint for high-risk manifest ops.
metadata.successSignals: Rewind restores working tree predictably; checkpoints listed per task; compare shows diff summary; tests cover stash and commit modes.
metadata.evidenceRef: docs/maintainers/runbooks/wishlist-workflow.md; maintainer wishlist batch 2026-04-02

wishlistConversionDecomposition.rationale: Split design and persistence from read-only CLI, then rewind with explicit safety rails, then optional auto-checkpoints so each slice is shippable and testable without one mega-diff.
wishlistConversionDecomposition.boundaries: In scope: git working-tree rollback points via stash or named commit pointers, per-task listing and compare, rewind that restores trees predictably, hooks for high-risk automation. Out of scope: cloud or VM snapshots, IDE-native checkpoint UX, destructive reflog rewriting.
wishlistConversionDecomposition.dependencyIntent: T748 locks the record model and store; T749 implements create/list/compare; T750 adds rewind and automated tests; T751 layers auto-checkpoint triggers after the manual commands exist.

tasks.title: Cursor 3 subagent for task-flow execution

metadata.problemStatement: Task delivery playbooks, maintainer-delivery-loop rules, and workspace-kit CLI steps are spread across many Cursor rules and docs. General-purpose agents load a large, overlapping rule surface, which increases context cost and makes it easy to skip or contradict task-engine policy (e.g. transitions vs chat-only claims).
metadata.expectedOutcome: A dedicated Cursor 3 subagent (or equivalent packaged agent profile) owns end-to-end task flow: queue inspection, run-transition discipline, branch/PR loop, and linked rules/skills co-located with that agent so the main session stays thinner. Where feasible, task-flow guidance is isolated from unrelated repo rules.
metadata.impact: Lower token load for non-task work, higher reliability on policy-sensitive kit commands, clearer handoff when someone explicitly wants task execution mode.
metadata.constraints: Must stay aligned with workspace-kit as source of truth; cannot replace policyApproval or CLI evidence; Cursor product capabilities (subagent packaging, rule scoping) may limit true isolation; avoid duplicating canonical docs in ways that drift.
metadata.successSignals: Documented subagent invocation pattern; scoped rule/skill bundle used in pilot sessions; fewer missed run-transition steps in maintainer delivery; optional metrics from team dogfooding.
metadata.evidenceRef: User request 2026-04-05 — abstract task flows into Cursor 3 subagent with task work and rules

wishlistConversionDecomposition.rationale: Wishlist bundles Cursor packaging research, concrete scoped artifacts (rules/skills/playbook pointers), and pilot evidence. Linear chain keeps design constraints frozen before implementation and avoids shipping dead config.
wishlistConversionDecomposition.boundaries: Does not change workspace-kit policy or replace policyApproval; Cursor product limits are documented rather than papered over; no wholesale duplication of .ai canon—only thin entry paths and scoped bundles.
wishlistConversionDecomposition.dependencyIntent: T745 blocks T746; T746 blocks T747.

Publish a short maintainer or **`.ai`**-adjacent reference (or extend an existing onboarding playbook) that maps prompt classes to **`workspace-kit run`** surfaces and feature slugs.

Cross-link **FEATURE-TAXONOMY** / **AGENT-CLI-MAP** instead of duplicating command catalogs.
Avoid stale prompt spam — version the list or mark it exemplar-only.
acceptanceCriteria: New agents can jump from ‘I want to do X’ to a supported command path without inventing invocations.
Improvement **`complete`** or **`cancelled`** with PR / note.

tasks.title: Plugin platform v1: Claude-layout reference plugin and CI validation
tasks.approach: Add a fixture plugin matching published Claude Code package layout; run workspace-kit list/inspect (and smoke enable path) in CI to prove cross-tool recognition modulo documented gaps.
technicalScope: Reference fixture under repo (e.g. docs/examples or test fixtures).
CI job step invoking workspace-kit against the fixture workspace.
Documentation tying fixture to Anthropic layout expectations.
acceptanceCriteria: CI loads fixture with same on-disk layout Claude Code documents (cited in ADR/fixture README).
list-plugins / inspect-plugin succeed in CI on the fixture.
Failures print actionable validation errors for maintainers.

tasks.title: Plugin platform v1: install, enable/disable, and doctor integration
tasks.approach: Add policy-gated mutating commands (install-plugin, enable-plugin, disable-plugin) persisting enablement state; surface effective plugin set in workspace-kit doctor output.
technicalScope: Persistence model for installed/enabled plugins (SQLite or config—follow existing kit patterns).
Policy approval or planning-generation hooks per repo standards for mutating run commands.
Doctor section summarizing roots, enabled plugins, and last validation status.
acceptanceCriteria: install-plugin can place or register a Claude-layout bundle per ADR boundaries.
enable/disable toggles affect command discovery deterministically.
doctor lists plugin health without silent failures on bad roots.

tasks.title: Plugin platform v1: read-only discovery (list-plugins, inspect-plugin)
tasks.approach: Implement task-engine commands to scan configured plugin roots, parse manifests with T684 schema, and emit stable JSON for agents—no install side effects.
technicalScope: New run commands: list-plugins, inspect-plugin (instruction md + router wiring).
Deterministic ordering and error surfaces for missing/invalid packages.
Reuse skills/subagents/hooks discovery patterns where practical.
acceptanceCriteria: list-plugins enumerates installed-layout packages under configured roots.
inspect-plugin returns manifest summary + validation diagnostics.
Contract tests cover happy path and representative malformed manifest.

tasks.title: Plugin platform v1: ADR, Claude Code manifest alignment, and configuration
tasks.approach: Study current Anthropic Claude Code plugin docs; write ADR with citations; add JSON Schema or equivalent validation artifact; introduce workspace-kit config for plugin roots and toggles; document WC-only extensions and explicit unsupported Claude capabilities.
technicalScope: ADR under docs/maintainers with official Anthropic plugin doc URLs.
Manifest fields for identity/version, commands, skills, subagents, hooks, permissions/compatibility—aligned to Claude where possible.
Config keys integrated with config-metadata / doctor visibility as appropriate.
Non-goals appendix for Claude runtime features WC does not implement in v1.
acceptanceCriteria: ADR merged with cited compatibility baseline.
Committed schema or validation module rejects malformed manifests deterministically.
Config documented via generated CONFIG path or module instructions; invalid config fails fast.
Published gap list for Claude-only behaviors (or explicit none).
